Declares a function to handle software failure resets without setting a reboot reason.

# Purpose
The code is a C header file that declares a function [`reset_due_to_software_failure`](<#reset_due_to_software_failure>). This function is intended to handle software failure scenarios by initiating a system reset. It uses the `__attribute__((noreturn))` to indicate that the function does not return to the caller. The function does not call `reboot_reason_set`; instead, it calls `reboot_reason_set_restarted_safely` if the system can shut down all processes safely before rebooting. The use of `#pragma once` ensures that the header file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
